Assembly: The assembly xbCerEdul1.1 is based on 42x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly icLorLunu1.1 is based on 29x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The X chromosome was identified based on synteny with Philonthus spinipes (GCA_963082785.1). The order and orientation of contigs in the following regions is uncertain: chromosome 2, 32.5 Mb to 36 Mb; chromosome 3, 27 Mb to 34 Mb; chromosome 4; chromosome 5, 27.8 Mb to 37.5 Mb; chromosome 6, 24.5 Mb to 34.5 Mb, 25.5 Mb to 40 Mb; chromosome 7, 25 Mb to 33 Mb; chromosome 8, 22 Mb to 32 Mb; chromosome 9, 23.7 Mb to 30 Mb; chromosome X, 25 Mb to 39 Mb.

Assembly: The assembly cmRicSoro2.1 is based on 51x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Order and Orientation of scaffolds on Chromosome 1 between ~9.69-9.94Mb, Chromosome 2 between ~45.63-46.24Mb, Chromosome 3 between ~12.17-12.65Mb and 39.46-39.86Mb, Chromosome 4 between ~17.22-18.20Mb, Chromosome 5 between ~17.96-18.24Mb and Chromosome 8 between ~5.17-5.58Mb is unsure.

Assembly: The assembly dmBerAqui1.hap1.1 is based on 49x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The following regions of this assembly consist of scaffolds in an uncertain order and orientation: Chromosome 3 ~35.5-43.9 Mbp, Chromosome 6 ~41.5-56.8 Mbp, Chromosome 8 ~31.6-39 Mbp, Chromosome 12 ~30.9-35.3 Mbp and Chromosome 14 ~16.4-23.7 Mbp. The following regions represent heterozygous inversions: Chromosome 1 ~23.1-34.5 Mbp (multiple), Chromosome 7 ~25-27 Mbp (single), Chromosome 9 ~34.3-37.8 Mbp (double) and Chromosome 11 ~27.3-29.7 (single) and ~44.3-47.1 Mbp (double).
